skill.name: process-code-review
skill.description: Process code review findings from review documents, apply selected fixes, run local quality checks, and update review status markers.
skill.summary: Guides interactive resolution of code review findings with tracked fixes, skips, and local validation checks.
skill.author: DiversioTeam

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many markdown backtick examples and keyword matches, but the high-risk C2 and weak cryptography findings are false positives in documentation context. The confirmed residual risk is legitimate local Bash execution and filesystem modification for applying code review fixes, including an auto-fix mode that should be used carefully.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution and File Modification Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally permits Bash and Edit usage, applies fixes to source files, updates review documents, and runs local tools such as ruff, ty, and python manage.py check. This is legitimate for a code review processing skill, but review document contents can influence which files are edited and which checks are executed, so users should review proposed changes before approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly authorizes Bash and Edit tools and instructs the assistant to run local quality commands after applying fixes. The context is a normal developer workflow, and I found no evidence of network access, credential access, obfuscation, or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Keyword Matches Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity static matches do not indicate malware. The C2 match is an example issue identifier, and the weak cryptography matches are ordinary documentation words such as description or table labels, not use of cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown examples and descriptive labels, not executable code or security-sensitive implementations. No evidence found of command-and-control behavior or weak cryptographic operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Auto-Fix Mode Can Apply Changes Without Per-Issue Prompts
dangerous_patterns.description: The --auto mode applies fixes without prompting for each issue. This supports efficient review processing, but it increases the impact of inaccurate review documents or unsafe suggested fixes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The auto mode is explicitly documented and can bypass per-issue confirmation. It is not malicious by itself, but it materially changes the approval model for file edits.

content.value_statement: Code review documents can contain many issues that are hard to process consistently. This skill organizes findings by severity, guides fixes or skips, runs local checks, and records status updates.
actual_capabilities: Parses severity-tagged review documents with BLOCKING, SHOULD_FIX, and NIT findings.
Presents review findings in priority order for fix or skip decisions.
Applies selected fixes to source files using local editing tools.
Runs local quality checks such as linting, formatting, type checks, and Django system checks.
Updates review documents with fixed, ignored, deferred, or will-not-fix status markers.
Supports dry-run, auto-fix, and severity filtering modes.
limitations: Requires a structured review document from the expected code review workflow.
Quality checks depend on local tools being installed in the target repository.
Auto-fix mode can apply unsuitable changes if the review document is inaccurate.
Does not commit changes or replace human review of security-sensitive fixes.

use_cases.title: Process Team Review Backlogs
use_cases.description: Work through a generated review document, address important findings first, and keep the review status current.

use_cases.title: Triage Release Blocking Issues
use_cases.description: Focus on BLOCKING findings before a merge or release while documenting any accepted deferrals.

use_cases.title: Handle Low-Risk Cleanup Efficiently
use_cases.description: Use severity filters or dry runs to process NIT and SHOULD_FIX items with controlled local validation.

prompt_templates.title: Start Interactive Processing
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin with a review document and approve each action manually.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use process-code-review on my review document. Show each issue in severity order and ask before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Preview Before Editing
prompt_templates.scenario: Inspect planned work without modifying files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a dry-run pass on this review document. Summarize the issues by severity and list the likely files affected.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Blocking Findings
prompt_templates.scenario: Resolve only the highest priority issues first.
prompt_templates.prompt: Process only BLOCKING findings from this review document. For each one, explain the fix and run relevant checks after editing.

prompt_templates.title: Controlled Auto-Fix Cleanup
prompt_templates.scenario: Apply low-risk fixes while keeping important findings interactive.
prompt_templates.prompt: Auto-fix only NIT findings from this review document, then summarize every changed file and any checks that failed.

output_examples.input: A review document with blocking security findings and several style nits.
output_examples.output: The skill presents the blocking items first, applies approved fixes, records skipped items with reasons, and summarizes modified files.

output_examples.input: A dry-run request for a generated API review file.
output_examples.output: The skill lists findings by severity, identifies likely affected files, and makes no edits.

output_examples.input: A request to process only minor cleanup findings.
output_examples.output: The skill filters to NIT items, applies approved low-risk changes, runs formatting checks, and updates status markers.

best_practices: Use interactive mode for security, data integrity, or tenant isolation findings.
Review proposed fixes before approving edits to production-critical code.
Run repository tests after the skill completes local linting and formatting checks.
anti_patterns: Do not use auto-fix mode on untrusted or poorly structured review documents.
Do not treat status markers as a substitute for verifying the changed code.
Do not skip BLOCKING issues without recording a clear reason and owner.

faq.question: What kind of input does this skill expect?
faq.answer: It expects a structured review document with severity tags, locations, problem descriptions, and proposed fixes.

faq.question: Does this skill change source files?
faq.answer: Yes. It can edit source files when the user approves fixes or when auto-fix mode is used.

faq.question: Can it run local commands?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to run local quality checks such as linting, formatting, type checks, and Django checks.

faq.question: Is auto-fix mode safe for every review?
faq.answer: No. Auto-fix mode should be limited to trusted review documents and low-risk findings.

faq.question: Does it commit code changes?
faq.answer: No. The skill updates files and review documents, but it does not create commits.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatible workflows.
